segments.text: The ice inside Neptune is hot, black,
and may cause the planet to rain
diamonds. Turns out ice has a bunch of
alien forms. And now we're making that
ice here on Earth. At its core, ice is
just a bunch of water molecules held
together by the hydrogen atoms. On
Earth, that typically looks like this,
neatly formed hexagons. But these
hydrogen bonds are pretty sensitive to
changes in pressure and temperature. So
if you mess with those two things, you
can get all kinds of cool alien ice. And
scientists love to mess with things. In
2019, they used diamonds and lasers to
put water molecules under extreme
temperature and pressure. And that
pressure compressed the oxygen atoms
into a lattice, making a solid. And the
extreme heat broke down the hydrogen
bonds, causing them to zoom around. And
all of that chaos stopped light from
getting through, making the ice look
super dark. And boom, Neptune's weird,
hot black ice here on Earth. But they
didn't stop there. That was ice 18. As
of 2025, they're up to ice 21. And every
new form is teaching us a little bit
more about the icy worlds away from
home. And we have a plan to drill into
one of those icy worlds.
